content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.6.1 / 5.6.3 WARN — RatingGateView.swift builds a custom star-rating gate that routes only 4-5 star ratings to a hardcoded `itms-apps://.../write-review` link while diverting lower ratings to a feedback form, and description.txt asks users to "rate us 5 stars"; no `SKStoreReviewController`/`requestReview` usage found anywhere.

Pierre: Apple explicitly disallows custom review prompts and gating write-review links based on a pre-screened rating (5.6.1) or soliciting only positive reviews (5.6.3/3.2.2 x). Here, `RatingGateView.swift:16-21` filters users by `stars >= 4` before sending them to the App Store review URL, which is a textbook review-manipulation dark pattern, and the "rate us 5 stars" copy compounds this. Replace this with `SKStoreReviewController.requestReview(in:)` (or `RequestReviewAction` in SwiftUI) called at a natural point in the app, without any pre-filtering by star rating, and remove the "rate us 5 stars" language from the description.
